title: 5 players the Celtics could target this offseason outside of Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Kawhi Leonard, subject to trade speculation, could be a key target for the Celtics to improve playoff contention.
LeBron James, a free agent, remains a potential fit for the Celtics despite personal ties likely keeping him in LA.
John Collins, averaging 13 points and shooting 40% from 3-point range last season, could add forward depth for Boston.

title: 2 local college baseball programs advance to NCAA World Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Bentley University advances to NCAA Division II World Series after a dramatic 3-2 victory over Molloy University.
Endicott College reaches NCAA Division III championship for the fourth consecutive year, defeating Salve Regina University.
Bentley and Endicott's game schedules for their respective World Series will be announced later.

title: Celtics reportedly plan to work out UConn basketball star ahead of NBA Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Celtics plan to work out UConn star forward Alex Karaban ahead of the 2026 NBA Draft.
Karaban, ranked No. 32 by ESPN, helped UConn win two NCAA championships and earned All-Big East First-team honors.
Celtics hold the No. 27 and No. 40 picks; Karaban averaged 12.5 points, 5.0 rebounds in 151 collegiate games.

title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth's 'Whatever's Clever! World Tour' concert at MGM Music Hall highlighted his blend of jazz and pop.
The show featured material from his March album and older hits, with audience interaction and a stripped-down stage setup.
Opening acts included Ally Salort and Daniel Seavey, both displaying musical versatility and strong performances.

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Wallflowers performed their entire 'Bringing Down the Horse' album at The Cabot in Beverly on May 22, 2026.
Following the album, they covered Tom Petty's 'Long After Dark' in full, highlighting Jakob Dylan's admiration for Petty's work.
The concert concluded with encores including 'Refugee' and 'The Waiting' by Tom Petty and Wallflowers' own songs.

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston saw the largest population decline in Massachusetts, losing 1,338 residents, continuing a trend since 2020.
Everett and Worcester experienced the biggest population gains, adding 2,362 and 1,916 residents respectively, according to the Census data.
Housing costs and reduced immigration contribute to slower statewide growth; Massachusetts' population rose by only 0.2% last year.

title: These were the Mass. companies on the new Forbes list of largest family businesses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Fidelity Investments and Demoulas Super Markets ranked on Forbes' largest family businesses list.
Fidelity, at No. 14, earned $37.7 billion; Demoulas Super Markets, at No. 69, earned $8 billion annually.
Forbes ranks family businesses by revenue; includes companies with significant founding family ownership.

title: UMass Amherst launches major housing revitalization project
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: UMass Amherst partners with American Campus Communities for a major campus housing overhaul to modernize facilities.
The project addresses student housing shortages and focuses on affordability, sustainability, and improved amenities.
Renovations will be phased to allow continued student residence, aligning with state housing priorities.

title: Review & Setlist: Lucinda Williams brings protest songs, fighting spirit to Medford
author: Terence Cawley
bullets: Lucinda Williams performed at Chevalier Theatre in Medford, showcasing songs from her new album "World's Gone Wrong".
Williams' performance featured protest songs addressing contemporary issues, including anti-Trump themes and social unrest.
The concert included covers of Bob Marley, Memphis Minnie, Skip James, and Neil Young, highlighting Williams' roots influences.

title: Twins unload on Justin Slaten as Red Sox lose 8-6 at Fenway Park
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox lost to the Twins 8-6 at Fenway Park, with Justin Slaten struggling on the mound.
Payton Tolle struck out nine in six innings, but was pulled after 85 pitches by manager Chad Tracy.
Justin Slaten allowed four hits, four runs, and two home runs in less than an inning during the Twins' comeback.
